requestBody.description: JSON payload containing the fields to be updated in the agreement.

200.description: The agreement has been successfully updated via document ID. Returns the complete updated agreement.
A JSON object containing details of the requested agreement document.

204.description: The agreement has been successfully deleted from the account.

requestBody.description: JSON payload containing the fields to be updated in the agreement.

200.description: The agreement has been successfully updated. Returns the complete updated agreement.

requestBody.description: JSON payload specifying the target agreement type.

200.description: The agreement type has been successfully changed. Returns the updated agreement.

change_type.description: Change the agreement type to a different configured type.

422.description: Unprocessable Entity - The specified type does not match any configured agreement type.

example.title: Invalid Agreement Type
example.detail: The agreement type 'InvalidType' is not configured for this account.

200.description: The bulk job status and details

job_name.description: Name for the new job to be created. If empty, server will auto-create name for the job.

expected_number_of_docs.description: Number of docs this job will have. Will use provided document_requests size if there is a mismatch.

language.description: Language for the user, such as en-US, en-GB, if not provided will default to en-US.

external_job_id.description: User provided reference ID for this job

name.description: User provided name for this job

message.example: Files exceeding 100MB or more than 10,000 per job will be rejected.

BulkJobEmbedded.description: Contains detailed information about the BulkJob including presigned upload links, document IDs, etc

documents.description: List of documents with presigned upload URLs

agreement_id.description: Agreement ID associated with this document, if created

file_name.description: The name of the uploaded file

error_code.description: Error code if document processing failed

error.description: Error message if document processing failed

_links.description: Hypermedia links related to this document

upload_document.description: Azure Blob Store presigned URL to upload the document file (valid for ~8 hours)

upload_metadata.description: Azure Blob Store url to upload metadata of the specific file

method.description: HTTP method used for this template

description.example: Upload document file to Azure Blob Storage

template_variables.description: Key value pairs of variable names and descriptions, explaining how they are to be used

headers.description: Key value pairs of header names and example values

error_status_codes.description: Key value pairs of error response codes and explanations of those codes

BulkJobConstraints.description: Describes the limits of a bulk job, or an action associated with a bulk job

BulkJobMetadata.description: Properties about THIS request/response, not the job resource itself

response_duration.description: Time in ms to process this request

DocStatus.description: Document status. Last 3 are terminal statuses. Matches enum with similar name in the backend.

BulkJobStatus.description:
Job lifecycle states:
* OPEN - Job created and accepting documents
* UPLOAD_COMPLETE - All documents have been received, and processing has not yet started
* IN_PROGRESS - Documents are being processed (AI extraction)
* COMPLETE - The full e2e process is complete
* FAILED - System error prevented job execution
* CANCELED - User initiated request to cancel a job

ErrDetails.description:
Error response conforming to RFC 9457 (Problem Details for HTTP APIs).
See: https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9457.html

type.description:
A URI reference that identifies the problem type. When dereferenced,
it should provide human-readable documentation for the problem type.
Use "about:blank" when no specific type documentation exists.

title.description: A short, human-readable summary of the problem type. Should not change between occurrences.

status.description: The HTTP status code generated by the origin server for this occurrence of the problem.

detail.description: A human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence of the problem.
detail.example: The provided 'email' parameter is not a valid email address.

instance.description: A URI reference that identifies the specific occurrence of the problem (typically the request path).

errors.description:
An array of detailed validation error objects when multiple issues exist.
Each entry identifies a specific field-level or parameter-level error.

request_id.description: Unique identifier for the request, useful for tracing and debugging with support.

timestamp.description: The timestamp when the error occurred.

ValidationError.description: A single field-level or parameter-level validation error.

code.description: A machine-readable error code identifying the specific validation failure.

message.description: A human-readable description of the validation error.
message.example: The provided email format is incorrect.

target.description: The name of the field, parameter, or path segment that caused the error.

ContinuationToken.description: The continuation token used to retrieve a page in a paginated response.

CustomProperty.description: A generic map/dict. The key is a string, and the value can be of any type, including strings, booleans, numbers, arrays, or objects

SuccessStatusCode.description:
Success status codes (2xx range) indicating the request was received,
understood, and accepted.
Common codes:
- 200: OK - Standard successful response
- 201: Created - Resource successfully created
- 202: Accepted - Request accepted for processing
- 204: No Content - Successful with no response body
- 206: Partial Content - Partial resource returned

LocalDateTime.description: ISO 8601 formatted date-time string. May be local (no timezone), UTC (Z suffix), or include an explicit offset (e.g., +05:30, -0800).

ResponseMetadata.description: Control information and metadata for the response.

PageLinks.description:
Hypermedia controls (HATEOAS) for navigating between pages in a paginated collection of results.
Links for the current page, next page, and previous page, with optional first and last page links.

PageLink.description:
A URL that references a specific page in the pagination process. This is typically used for navigation
between paginated results.

href.description: The URL for the referenced page.

PageLimit.description: The maximum number of items that can be returned in a single page.

created_at.description: Timestamp when the agreement document was created.

created_by.description: User ID of the person who created the agreement document.

modified_at.description: Timestamp when the agreement document was last modified.

modified_by.description: User ID of the person who last modified the agreement document.

ResourceSourceAccountId.description: The Account ID of the source system who creates this entity, e.g. eSign Account ID

ResourceSourceId.description: The ID of the entity in the source system that this entity is associated with. For example, it could be an ID of the envelope in eSign.

ResourceSourceName.description: The name of the source system who creates this entity, e.g. eSign, CLM, or Salesforce.

LinkedData.description: Identifies records in external systems which correlate with this agreement.

RequestId.description: Unique identifier for the request, useful for tracking and debugging.

ResponseTimestamp.description: The timestamp indicating when the response was generated.

ResponseDurationMs.description:
The duration of time, in milliseconds, that the server took to process and respond
to the request. This is measured from the time the server received the request
until the time the response was sent.

Agreement.description:
The Agreement component represents a comprehensive overview of a contractual document, detailing its unique identifiers, key properties, parties involved,
and specific provisions. It includes general information such as the title, type, status, and important dates like effective and expiration dates.
The component also incorporates various provisions—legal, financial, lifecycle, and custom—along with metadata, external references, and related documents
to offer a full representation of the structure and context of an agreement.

id.description: The unique identifier for the agreement document.

title.description: Title of the agreement document, summarizing its purpose.

file_name.description: The file name of the agreement.

document_id.description: The id the original agreement document.

category.description: Server-defined category based on the agreement type.

summary.description: A detailed summary of the agreement's key provisions and scope.
summary.example: This Master Service Agreement between Alpha Corp and Beta Ltd. defines the terms for services provided by Alpha Corp, including project scope, payment terms, and dispute resolution.

status.description: Current status of the agreement (e.g., PENDING, COMPLETE, INACTIVE)

review_status.description: The review status of the agreement, indicating whether it has been complete or pending.

review_completed_at.description: The date when the agreement extraction review was completed.

parties.description: A list of parties involved in the agreement.

provisions.description: "The conditions or rules written in a legal agreement. The set of possible provisions is determined by the agreement type."

custom_provisions.description:
“A dictionary of user-defined provisions that extend the standard provisions set for this agreement type.
  • Keys = names of additional terms supplied by the customer (e.g., early_termination_fee).
  • Values = the term’s value, expressed as a primitive JSON type (string, number, boolean, etc.).
This object reflects the data model as-defined by the customer, including property names and data types.
note - Changes to the underlying data model will be reflected dynamically in this object at runtime.

additional_user_defined_data.description: User-defined/custom attributes included in this agreement.

additional_custom_clm_data.description: Custom attributes originating from CLM in this agreement.

additional_custom_esign_data.description: Custom attributes originating from eSign in this agreement.

parent_agreement_document_id.description: ID of the parent agreement document, if related.

languages.description: List of languages applicable to the agreement, identified using BCP-47 language codes.

source_name.description: The name of the source system which created this agreement, e.g. eSign, CLM, or Salesforce.

source_id.description: The ID of the resource in the source system associated with this agreement. For example, it could be an ID of the envelope in eSign.

source_account_id.description: The Account ID of the source system who creates this entity, e.g. eSign Account ID

AgreementActions.description: Available actions on the agreement. Actions are conditionally present based on the current state of the resource.

change_type.description:
Change the agreement type to a different configured type.
Only present when the agreement type is mutable.

href.description: The URL for the change-type action.

method.description: The HTTP method for the action.

description.example: Change the agreement type to a different configured type.

AgreementLinks.description: Hypermedia controls (HATEOAS) for agreement specific links to resources.

agreement_types.description: Link to the collection of configured agreement types for the account. Always present.

Link.description: A URL that references a specific resource.

href.description: The URL for the referenced page.

AgreementSummary.description: A high-level, AI-generated summary of an agreement document.

summary.description:
An AI-generated summary of the agreement. This summary is provided for convenience
and may not capture every detail of the original agreement.

AnnualAgreementValue.description:
The AnnualAgreementValue component captures the financial details of the agreement, including the annualized value and currency code.
It provides a clear overview of the agreement's annualized financial worth, aiding in financial planning and assessment.

annual_agreement_value.description: Total annual value of the agreement.

annual_agreement_value_currency_code.description: Currency code (e.g., USD, EUR) for the agreement's annual value.

Assignment.description:
Assignment specifies the rules and conditions regarding the transfer of rights or obligations under the agreement. This includes the type of assignment allowed,
how changes in control of the assigning party are handled, and conditions for the termination of assignment rights, providing clarity on when and how rights can be transferred or reassigned.

AssignmentType.description: The type of assignment rights in the agreement (e.g., transferability)

AssignmentChangeOfControl.description: Provisions related to changes in control of the assigning party

AssignmentTerminationRights.description: Provisions for the termination of assignment rights

ConfidentialityObligationPeriod.description: A subset of ISO 8601 duration. Fractional or negative values are not supported.

EffectiveDate.description: The date when the terms of the agreement start to apply and become legally binding.

ExecutionDate.description: The date when the agreement is signed by all parties, making it officially binding. This is not necessarily the same as the effective date.

ExpirationDate.description: The date when the agreement ends and is no longer valid or enforceable.

FinancialProvisions.description:
'Financial Provisions outlines the financial terms and conditions of the agreement, including the annual and total value of the agreement, liability limits, and payment-related details such as terms,
potential late fees, and price adjustment caps. These provisions govern the monetary aspects of the agreement, defining how payments are managed, and establishing financial limits and obligations.'

GeneralProvisions.description:
The GeneralProvisions component defines the fundamental terms and key dates of the agreement, including when it starts, when it has been executed, when it expires, and the overall duration.
These provisions set the basic framework for the agreement, establishing its timeline and primary milestones.

GoverningLaw.description: The governing law clause identifies the substantive law that will govern the rights and obligations of the parties to the agreement.

Jurisdiction.description: A jurisdiction clause expressly sets out which courts or tribunals have the power to hear a dispute which arises under the agreement.

LatePaymentFee.description:
The LatePaymentFee component defines the conditions under which fees can be charged for late payments, including whether such fees are allowed and the percentage rate applied.
This component helps outline the financial penalties for overdue payments, encouraging timely compliance with payment terms.

can_charge_late_payment_fees.description: Indicates if late payment fees can be charged.

late_payment_fee_percent.description: Percentage fee charged on late payments.

LegalProvisions.description:
The LegalProvisions component encompasses clauses and terms that define the legal rights, responsibilities, and protections of the parties involved in an agreement.
This includes standard legal aspects such as confidentiality, governing law, jurisdiction, and other regulatory or compliance-related provisions.

LiabilityCap.description:
The LiabilityCap component specifies the maximum financial liability that a party can incur under the agreement, including the cap amount, the applicable currency,
any multiplier used to calculate the cap, and the duration for which the cap is effective. This serves to limit the financial exposure and risk for the parties involved.

liability_cap_fixed_amount.description: Maximum liability cap in the agreement

liability_cap_currency_code.description: Currency code for the liability cap amount.

liability_cap_multiplier.description: Multiplier applied to calculate the liability cap

LifecycleProvisions.description:
'The LifecycleProvisions component outlines the terms and conditions related to the duration and management of an agreement, including how the agreement can be renewed, extended, or terminated.
These provisions govern the different phases of the agreement's lifecycle, detailing how it progresses, concludes, or continues.'

NdaType.description: Type of non-disclosure agreement (e.g., unilateral, bilateral).

Party.description: A party is a person, group, or organization that is involved in a contract and has legally binding obligations and responsibilities. They also seek to benefit from the agreement.

id.description: Unique identifier for the party, mapped to the party entity reference.

name_in_agreement.description: Name of the party as it appears in the agreement.

payment_terms_due_date.description: Terms specifying the payment due date, based on a defined number of days or other conditions.

price_cap_percent_increase.description: Maximum allowed percentage increase in prices, limited between 0 and 100.

Renewal.description:
The Renewal component defines the terms and conditions for extending the duration of the agreement, including the type of renewal, notice period, and the length of the renewal term.
It covers aspects such as whether the renewal is automatic or requires action, the timeframe for giving notice, and the responsibilities and additional information regarding the renewal process.

renewal_type.description: Specifies the type of renewal (e.g., automatic, manual).

renewal_notice_period.description: The period of time that a party is required to provide to indicate their intention to renew an agreement.

renewal_notice_date.description: Calculated field based on renewal notice period. (agreement expiration date - renewal notice period duration)

auto_renewal_term_length.description: The duration of the auto-renewal period.

renewal_extension_period.description: The period an agreement has been extended after it has been renewed.

renewal_process_owner.description: User ID of the person responsible for managing the renewal process

renewal_additional_info.description: Additional information related to the renewal process.

Termination.description:
The Termination component outlines the conditions and timeframes under which an agreement can be ended, including specific durations for termination due to non-performance or for cause.
This helps define the process and notice periods required for legally ending the agreement.

TerminationForCause.description:
The specific duration that a party has to give notice before terminating the agreement due to a significant breach or violation of terms.
This period allows the other party to address the cause or prepare for termination.

TerminationForConvenience.description: Specifies the required notice period that a party must provide before terminating the agreement for convenience, without cause, under the terms outlined in the contract.

TotalAgreementValue.description:
The TotalAgreementValue captures the financial details of an agreement expressed as its total value and unit of currency.
It provides a clear overview of the agreement's total financial worth, aiding in financial planning and assessment.

total_agreement_value_currency_code.description: Currency code (e.g., USD, EUR) for the agreement's total value.

TextRange.description: A span of text within an agreements.

matched_text.description: The text from within the document where the extracted value was derived.

extracted_value.description: The value extracted from the matched_text.

value_type.description: The data type of the extracted value.

Affordance.description:
Affordances (aka 'actions') describe the available operations on a resource, including CRUD and RPC-like operations. It details the
expected input payload, http method, query parameters, and the resulting output.

Affordances enable clients to dynamically adapt to the API's current state and available actions. Instead of
hardcoding all possible endpoints and their associated logic, a client can inspect the affordances within a
resource's representation to discover what actions are possible and how to perform them.

href.description: A URI representing the location of the resource or operations.

method.description: The http method to use when invoking this affordance

required.description:
Indicates if an action must be performed on a resource. This may include a manadatory step as part of a business process, especially
when failing to execute a subsequent action will leave the resource in a bad or incomplete state.
# note: would it be necessary for manadatory/required actionsto be performed prior to an optional action
# note: mandatory actions which have previously been executed on a resource should not be be returned in the server response
# execept in cases where the resource is in a state that permits this action to be performed again.

sequence.description:
Provides a stable and consistent order to a collection of resources. Useful when multiple processes are working in parallel to iterate over
a collection of resources with a mandatory/required action.

template_variables.description: A natural language dictionary of JSON template variables used in invoke an action

headers.description: Additional HTTP request headers needed in the request to invoke an action.

AgreementTypeListResponse.description: List of configured agreement type name strings.

ChangeAgreementTypeRequest.description: Request body for changing an agreement's type.

type.description: The name of the target agreement type from the agreement-types collection.

ChangeAgreementTypeResponse.description: The updated agreement after a type change.

category.description: Server-recomputed category based on new type.

BadRequest.description: Bad Request - The request could not be understood or was missing required parameters.

example.title: Bad Request
example.detail: The request could not be understood or was missing required parameters.

Forbidden.description: Forbidden - The request was valid, but you do not have permission to perform the action.

example.title: Forbidden
example.detail: You do not have permission to perform the requested action.

example.title: Internal Server Error
example.detail: An unexpected error occurred on the server.

NotFound.description: Not Found - The requested resource could not be found.

example.title: Not Found
example.detail: The requested resource could not be found.

Unauthorized.description: Unauthorized - Authentication is required and has failed or not been provided.

example.title: Unauthorized
example.detail: Authentication is required and has failed or not been provided.

BadRequestError.description: Bad Request - The request was invalid or malformed

ForbiddenError.description: Forbidden - The user is not authorized to perform this operation

example.title: Forbidden
example.detail: The user is not authorized to perform this operation. Connect to your DS admin.

NotFoundError.description: Not Found - The requested resource was not found

RequestTimeoutError.description: Request Timeout - The request did not complete in time

PayloadTooLargeError.description: Payload Too Large - File size exceeded

UnsupportedMediaTypeError.description: Unsupported Media Type - File is not supported

UnprocessableEntityError.description: Unprocessable Entity - File is corrupted or unreadable

TooManyRequestsError.description: Too Many Requests - User has too many incomplete jobs

example.title: Too Many Requests
example.detail: User has too many incomplete jobs

InternalServerError2.description: Internal Server Error - An unexpected error occurred

AgreementTypeListResponse.description: A list of configured agreement types for the account.

PageLimit.name: limit
PageLimit.description: The maximum number of items that can be returned in a single page.

CToken.name: ctoken
CToken.description: An opaque token that helps retrieve the a page of data.

ListProperty.name: property
ListProperty.description: The name of the property for which to get valid values. Supported values are `status`, `assignment-type`, `assignment-termination-rights`, `nda-type`, and `renewal-type`.

ODataFilter.name: $filter
ODataFilter.description:
OData filter expression for complex queries. Supports:
- Comparison operators: `eq`, `ne`, `gt`, `ge`, `lt`, `le`
- Logical operators: `and`, `or`
- In operator: `in` (e.g., `type in ('Msa','Sow')`)

**Note**: Use forward slash `/` to navigate nested properties (e.g., `provisions/effective_date`), not dot notation.

Examples:
- `status eq 'COMPLETE' and provisions/effective_date ge 2025-01-01`
- `parties/name_in_agreement eq 'Acme Corp' or parties/name_in_agreement eq 'Beta Ltd'`
- `provisions/renewal_type in ('EVERGREEN','AUTO_RENEW')`

BulkJobResponseExample.description: Example response for a successfully created bulk upload job

value.name: test_name

template_variables.document_name: Client-provided filename, used in x-ms-meta-filename header

value.message: Files exceeding 100MB or more than 10,000 per job will be rejected.

BulkJobStatusExample.description: Example response for getting bulk job status with completed processing

AgreementSummaryExample.description: A simple example of an agreement summary response.

value.summary: This is a sample summary of an agreement.

accessToken.description:
OAuth2 authentication.
- Demo:
  - authorizationUrl: https://account-d.docusign.com/oauth/auth
  - tokenUrl: https://account-d.docusign.com/oauth/token
- Production:
  - authorizationUrl: https://account.docusign.com/oauth/auth
  - tokenUrl: https://account.docusign.com/oauth/token

scopes.adm_store_unified_repo_read: Required to read agreement data from the Agreement Manager API.
scopes.adm_store_unified_repo_write: Required to write agreement data to the Agreement Manager API.
scopes.models_read: Used to the read model schema. Not currently required for any API endpoint methods, but it should be requested by apps making the Agreement Manager API calls to support forward-compatibility, as a best practice.
scopes.document_uploader_write: Required for the createBulkUploadJob() and UploadComplete.
scopes.public_dms_document_read: Required to call the download_url property of an agreement object so you can download the agreement.
